offensive.name: Alchemical Scar
offensive.description: You brand the target with pain that reactivates over time.
tiers: On a failed save, the target takes acid damage equal to your spellcasting modifier at the start of each of their turns for 1 minute.
The ongoing acid damage increases to 2d6, and affected targets are considered flammable until the end of your next turn.
The target gains a Scar Marker — for 1 minute, every attack that hits them deals an additional 1d6 acid damage as the scar reopens under impact.
The scar becomes permanent unless removed by Greater Restoration or Regenerate. A creature bearing a permanent scar takes double damage from all Acid Cognition effects.

control.name: Corrode
control.description: Armor rusts, scales pit, runes fade.
tiers: The target takes −2 AC until the end of your next turn as acid eats into their defenses.
The AC penalty becomes cumulative — each failed save against your Acid effects adds an additional −1 AC (maximum −5 total). Resets after a short rest.
Each time the target fails a save against your Acid effects, they also lose one damage resistance of your choice for 1 hour.
On a failed save, the target's armor or natural armor is temporarily nullified for 1 minute — AC is calculated as if unarmored, and any AC bonus from shields is halved.

control.name: Dissolve the Arcane
control.description: Acid eats not only flesh — but formulas.
tiers: Targets concentrating on a spell have disadvantage on Constitution saving throws to maintain Concentration when affected by this effect.
On a failed save, a concentrating creature must make a second Constitution save or their spell immediately ends.
On a failed save, the creature also forgets the dissolved spell until they finish a long rest.
On a failed save, the concentration effect is destroyed entirely and triggers arcane backlash — the caster takes 5d6 psychic damage and must succeed on a Wisdom save or be Silenced for 1 minute.

control.name: Melting Grasp
control.description: What once stood strong becomes slop.
tiers: The effect deals double damage to nonmagical objects, ignoring object resistance.
Magical objects in contact with the effect must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or lose one magical property for 1 minute.
Constructs and animated objects are Stunned for 1 round and vulnerable to all damage while affected.

Choose one magical or nonmagical item within range — on a failed save, the item is permanently melted or dissolved, losing its function entirely until reforged or purified by divine means. Artifacts are immune.

control.name: Rot the Core
control.description: You corrupt not just the surface — but what lies beneath.
tiers: The target makes their next saving throw against Poison or Disease at disadvantage.
The target loses immunity to Poison and Disease for 1 minute.
The target is Contaminated — suffering −2 to all Constitution saving throws and treated as Diseased for the purposes of all spell effects for 1 minute.
The target becomes a walking vector for 1 minute — whenever they fail a Constitution saving throw, all creatures within 10 feet must also save or be afflicted with the same condition.

control.name: Unmake
control.description: You dissolve enchantments like fat in flame.
tiers: If the effect hits a creature under a magical defense (such as Mage Armor or Shield of Faith), that defense is suppressed for 1 round.
Magical effects of 3rd level or lower are automatically suppressed on a failed save.
Suppressed magical effects are dissolved rather than paused — the caster must re-cast them from scratch.

Attempt to erase one ongoing magical effect as if using a 9th-level Dispel Magic. You may also render one magic item inactive for 1 minute — on a second exposure, the item is permanently disabled until repaired by a master artificer or divine restoration.

complementEffects.name: Caustic Residue
complementEffects.description: The acid doesn't stop when the spell does.
complementEffects.effect: On a hit or failed saving throw, the target takes acid damage equal to your spellcasting modifier + proficiency bonus at the start of their next turn.
upgrades: Level 5+: Residue persists for 1 minute. Target may spend their action to wipe it away with a successful Constitution save vs your Verum DC.
Level 11+: Residue also reduces the target's AC by 1 for each turn it remains active, stacking with any existing Corrode penalties.

complementEffects.name: Corrosive Veil
complementEffects.description: Touch the caster, and the acid answers.
complementEffects.effect: The next creature that hits you with a melee attack before the start of your next turn takes acid damage equal to your spellcasting modifier and has their AC reduced by 1 until the end of their next turn.
upgrades: Level 5+: Damage increases to your spellcasting modifier + proficiency bonus, and the veil persists for 1 minute.
Level 11+: AC reduction increases to −2, and creatures that take this damage must succeed on a Constitution save or have one piece of worn or carried equipment suppressed for 1 round.

complementEffects.name: Dissolution Field
complementEffects.description: The ground itself begins to corrode.
complementEffects.effect: Acid spreads in a 10-foot radius around the target — becoming difficult terrain for 1 minute. Any nonmagical object remaining in contact with the ground there takes 1d6 acid damage at the end of each turn.
upgrades: Level 5+: Radius increases to 20 feet. Creatures that start their turn in the field or enter it take 1d4 acid damage.
Level 11+: Field becomes self-sustaining (no Concentration). Armor worn by creatures in the field takes −1 AC per minute of contact, resetting after cleaning during a short rest.
